If I had to compare Palworld to Pokémon, Palworld is better in most ways but it has big problems.

For a "Pokémon knock-off", this is heavily overpriced and not worth that amount. If it was +-10€, even if 15€, I wouldn't say a word.
The game is enjoyable for few hours and then the will to play is gone for the rest of the game.
Imo survival and farming is a big miss, especially if the Pals, even if good are stupidly incapable of farming which make it then extremely annoying to even touch that feature again. 
Dying should be toggled off till Hard Difficulty.
Saddles are limited to few Pals, therefore you cannot even ride your favourite ones even if they could be rideable/flyable.
98% of quests are meaningless and won't help you in anything.
I love however the world building in this game and I had a blast exploring.

I'd still rather stick to Pokémon than this in honesty.

Stupid behaviour system. You can do everything perfectly, play perfectly, you can be on a winning streak of 13+ games, and still get mass voted by clan salts, and lose 1000 behaviour per game even though you win the game. A game where u can do nothing wrong, no toxic chat, no toxic behaviour and still lose score just because someone else was salty. Pathetic devs excuse to keep their friends safe from new comers. 

I lost 1000 points in a game where I went 22/2/23. Eventually you can't even play ranked because the bahaviour system doesn't care for the reason where someone reported you or if it is valid, you just lose points if a clan reports you all at once.
yes i recommend but if there was a better anticheat and less cheaters

I’m waiting for Subnautica 2, so I ended up playing Palworld.

Palworld: Wtf is this monster?
Subnautica: Wtf is this monster?

Palworld: Why did I just drop this rock?
Subnautica: Why did I just find this rock?

Palworld: Why is there glitter on the water?
Subnautica: Why is there magma under the water?

Palworld: Oh, a mountain. Time to build a base.
Subnautica: Oh, land. Time to build a base.

Different vibes. Same confusion.

I miss Subnautica…
